pi_items.source: Task setup script and Kaggle dataset description confirm the task uses the fictional IBM HR Analytics Employee Attrition dataset.
Python generation script in setup_task.sh populates exactly 1470 employee records (63 HR + 961 R&D + 446 Sales).
Python generation script explicitly generates exactly 237 'Yes' attrition records (12 HR + 133 R&D + 92 Sales).
Calculated directly from the generated totals: 237 / 1470 = 0.161224...
Python generation script defines and generates rows strictly for these three department names.
Checked export_result.sh which looks for the file at this specific path to report completion.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically generated fictional dataset mimicking the IBM HR Analytics Employee Attrition dataset. It has been verified via the setup_task.sh script that the environment generates exactly 1,470 employee records across three departments (Human Resources, Research & Development, Sales). The total number of attrition ('Yes') cases is exactly 237, resulting in an overall attrition rate of approximately 0.1612 (16.12%). The expected final output is verified to be an Excel workbook saved to /home/ga/Documents/Spreadsheets/workforce_analytics.xlsx.
